/home/erik/.local/bin/ddx
ID:       ddx-70a432b5
Title:    Story 16.3: Specs + cross-cutting e2e + raw-transcript-viewed audit event
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   ac-quality:needs-refinement, phase:2, story:16
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-a8a18cb7
Deps:     ddx-f04faa86, ddx-4cd64068, ddx-90e6d29d, ddx-18d83a6a, ddx-b4c5151a
Desc:     PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-*.md: missing a Run-detail-page subsection describing the trust boundary ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args') and evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-*.md: Run resolver contract does not include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, or normalized ToolCallEntry.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-*.md: review surface does not point to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- No 'raw transcript viewed' audit event exists in the server or schema.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (16.1 page) and ddx-4cd64068 (16.2 evidence tab).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-008: add Run-detail-page subsection with trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- Add 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server (emitted on prompt/response/transcript access).
- Add cross-cutting e2e: full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end via Playwright.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (that is Story 16.1 and 16.2).
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.
Accept:   1. FEAT-008 spec updated with Run-detail-page subsection + trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist.
2. FEAT-010 spec updated with extended Run resolver contract + drain-time persistence note + ToolCallEntry definition.
3. FEAT-019 spec updated with link from review surface to /runs/[runId].
4. 'raw transcript viewed' audit event emitted on access and covered by test (TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed).
5. Cross-cutting e2e test exercises full prompt visible + response visible + tool trace visible + evidence list visible: test('full stack: prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence', ...) Playwright test.
6. p95 dataset (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files) all tabs render and become interactive within one render frame.
7. bun run test:e2e green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

ID:       ddx-b4c5151a
Title:    frontend: verify Story 16 run-detail p95 tab interactivity
Type:     task

Desc:     PROBLEM
Story 16.3 part 3 needs the run-detail page to stay responsive with a p95-shaped fixture: 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files. The prior bead text accidentally included copied parent scope for FEAT-008/FEAT-010/FEAT-019 spec updates, server audit events, and broader cross-cutting e2e work, but this child owns only the p95 frontend/e2e verification slice.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts:1164 defines the intended `Story16RunDetailFullStackP95` Playwright scenario.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts:1367 defines the one-render-frame interaction guard via `requestAnimationFrame` and a `<100ms` assertion.
- The previous decomposition copied parent context into this child, leaving the title/description broader than the acceptance criteria.

PROPOSED FIX
- Keep this bead scoped to the `Story16RunDetailFullStackP95` Playwright coverage.
- Verify the prompt, response, tools, and evidence tabs render expected content for a fixture with 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files.
=== ddx-17388615 ===
ID:       ddx-17388615
Title:    docs: normalize Story 16 run-detail specs across FEAT-008/010/019
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:16, ac-quality:needs-refinement, area:docs, kind:spec, spec:FEAT-008, spec:FEAT-010, spec:FEAT-019
Parent:   ddx-70a432b5
Deps:     ddx-70a432b5
Desc:     PROBLEM
Story 16.3 needs the run-detail page, evidence whitelist, resolver contract, and review deep-link documented together. The current docs already cover parts of that surface, but the wording is split across three specs and still needs to be normalized into one coherent Story 16.3 contract.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md:835-841 documents the run-detail evidence whitelist and `run_detail_view`, but it does not present the Story 16.3 trust-boundary language as a dedicated subsection.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:1215-1236 defines `run_detail_view` audit semantics, but the run resolver contract and drain-time persistence notes need to be spelled out in the same place.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md:64-73 ties evaluation drill-down to the shared run-detail surface, but the review surface still needs an explicit `/runs/[runId]` deep-page link.

PROPOSED FIX
- FEAT-008: add or normalize the Run-detail-page subsection so the trust boundary and evidence whitelist are explicit.
- FEAT-010: extend the run resolver contract text with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path), bundle-root confinement, and normalized ToolCallEntry persistence at drain time.
- FEAT-019: point the review surface at `/runs/[runId]` so the deep page is called out directly.
=== ddx-1664e1d2 ===
ID:       ddx-1664e1d2
Title:    server: add raw-transcript audit event for run-detail access
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:16, ac-quality:needs-refinement, area:server, kind:fix, spec:FEAT-010
Parent:   ddx-70a432b5
Deps:     ddx-70a432b5
Desc:     PROBLEM
Story 16.3 needs an audit event when raw transcript content is accessed, but the current run resolver only records `run_detail_view` on canonical detail loads. There is no dedicated raw-transcript access event or test coverage for that behavior.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go:100-145 appends `run_detail_view` from `recordRunDetailView`, and that hook is only called from `Run`; prompt/response/tool/evidence access does not emit a distinct raw-transcript event.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs_test.go:332-357 covers the existing `run_detail_view` behavior, but there is no `TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed` coverage.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a dedicated raw-transcript audit event path in the runs GraphQL/server layer.
- Emit the event when prompt/response/transcript data is accessed, not on tab switches or unrelated navigation.
- Add `TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed` covering the new event and its body fields.

=== ddx-f7c9159f ===
ID:       ddx-f7c9159f
Title:    frontend: add full-stack run-detail Playwright coverage and p95 render-frame check
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:16, ac-quality:needs-refinement, area:frontend, kind:test, spec:FEAT-008, spec:FEAT-010
Parent:   ddx-70a432b5
Deps:     ddx-70a432b5
Desc:     PROBLEM
The run-detail page and evidence browser need a single end-to-end regression test that exercises prompt, response, tool trace, and evidence together, plus the p95 dataset size check. The current Playwright coverage only checks the evidence tab slice.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs-evidence.spec.ts:241-305 currently covers evidence tab behavior, but it does not assert the complete prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence flow in one scenario or the 200-tool-call/30-file render-frame budget.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add one Playwright scenario named `full stack: prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence`.
- Cover prompt visible, response visible, tool trace visible, and evidence list visible states in a single run-detail flow.
- Add the p95 dataset fixture/check for 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files and keep the tabs interactive within one render frame.

NON-SCOPE

input.content:
status: blocked
reason: parent of already-decomposed open children; depth-2 split cap reached

This bead has been decomposed twice. Round 1 produced ddx-90e6d29d,
ddx-18d83a6a, ddx-b4c5151a (all closed). Round 2 produced the current
open children (per the bead's own notes field):

- ddx-17388615 — docs: normalize Story 16 run-detail specs across FEAT-008/010/019 (open)
- ddx-1664e1d2 — server: add raw-transcript audit event for run-detail access (open)
- ddx-f7c9159f — frontend: add full-stack run-detail Playwright coverage and p95 render-frame check (open)

Per the execute-bead Step-0 guidance, "Auto-decomposition is capped at
depth 2." The parent's 8 ACs already map cleanly onto those three open
children:

- AC1, AC2, AC3 (FEAT-008/010/019 spec text) → ddx-17388615
- AC4 (TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed) → ddx-1664e1d2
- AC5, AC6, AC7, AC8 (full-stack Playwright + p95 + test:e2e + lefthook) → ddx-f7c9159f

The pre-claim intake warning on this bead (kind=intake.warn,
readiness_best_effort) also flagged that the bulk of the requested
documentation and audit-event work already exists in the repository:

- FEAT-008 Run-detail evidence whitelist + run_detail_view audit event:
  docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md:835-841
- FEAT-010 run_detail_view body schema:
  docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:1215-1236
- FEAT-019 review surface deep-link to run-detail:
  docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md:64-83, 377-383
- Server emit + test:
  cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go:29-145
  cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs_test.go:340-357
- Evidence-tab Playwright coverage:
  cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs-evidence.spec.ts:253-303

Executing the parent directly would either (a) duplicate work already
covered by the open children, or (b) require a third-level split, which
Step-0 explicitly forbids. The right next move is to drain the three
open children; this parent should remain blocked on them.
